methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Amperon is a Series B infrastructure and logistics company founded by Sean Kelly that has raised $20M to date. The comparable universe for this sector is sparse; only one private peer (AiDash) exists in the Infrastructure & Logistics space, insufficient to support a robust peer-median valuation approach. No public comparables were identified.

Given the narrow peer set, stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation signal. Using total raised ($20M) as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $38M. This method is the only quantitatively defensible approach available given data constraints.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $32M to $44M, with a median of $38M. The range reflects inherent uncertainty in applying a single method rather than convergence across multiple independent approaches. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier for this company's specific risk profile and growth trajectory, the reliance on total raised as a valuation proxy without visibility into prior round prices, and the lack of sector-specific comps to validate assumptions. Expansion of the peer set or disclosure of prior round valuations would strengthen conviction in this range.
